title: BLIP-2: Bootstrapping Language-Image Pre-training with Frozen Image Encoders and Large Language Models
hook: BLIP-2 lets vision and language models talk without retraining either from scratch.
promise: You will understand how BLIP-2 connects image encoders and language models for efficient multimodal learning.
prerequisite_note: You only need a basic sense of how AI models process images and text to get started.

glossary.plain: A model whose weights are fixed and not updated during training.
A neural network trained on lots of text to understand and generate language.
A lightweight module that connects vision and language models by translating features between them.
Training models to understand and relate information from different sources, like images and text.
Training a model on a broad dataset before fine-tuning it for specific tasks.
Applying a model to new tasks it was not specifically trained on, without extra training.

cards.title: A bridge between vision and language
body: Discover how BLIP-2 connects frozen image and language models for smarter multimodal AI.

cards.title: How do models see and talk?
body: Imagine an AI that can describe a picture in perfect detail or answer questions about it. This requires understanding both images and language.
Traditionally, models for images and text are trained separately, so combining them is tricky and expensive. BLIP-2 offers a shortcut that makes this combination much easier.

cards.title: How BLIP-2 links vision and language
body: BLIP-2 starts with two powerful but frozen models: one for images and one for language. They do not speak the same language at first.
A lightweight query transformer learns to translate between these two worlds, letting the models collaborate without retraining either one.
cards.caption: BLIP-2 glues two expert models together using a small, trainable bridge.

cards.title: Training from scratch vs. BLIP-2's bridge
body: Suppose you want your AI to both see and talk. Should you train everything together or connect strong models with a bridge?
cards.offNote: Needs huge data and compute. Harder to reuse existing models.
cards.onNote: Keeps image and language experts frozen. Only learns a small connector.

cards.title: The trick: bridging with a Query Transformer
body: BLIP-2 does not retrain large vision or language models. Instead, it adds a compact query transformer that learns to translate image features into language features.
This lets the image encoder and language model remain untouched, while the query transformer adapts their outputs to work together on multimodal tasks.

cards.title: How much do you freeze?
body: Adjust how much of the system you keep frozen versus how much you retrain.
cards.low: Full flexibility but huge cost and risk of overfitting.
cards.mid: Retrain only the bridge. Good balance of efficiency and adaptability.
cards.high: No adaptation possible. Models cannot talk to each other.

cards.title: Pick your BLIP-2 ingredients
body: Which parts do you need to assemble a BLIP-2 style system?

choices.label: Query Transformer
choices.note: Learns to connect vision and language features.

choices.label: Image-Text Pretraining Data
choices.note: Pairs of images and text for learning the bridge.

choices.label: Linear Projection Layer
choices.note: Maps features into a common space before the bridge.

choices.label: Task-specific Head
choices.note: Optional. Adapts the output for classification or generation tasks.

cards.title: BLIP-2's surprising performance
body: BLIP-2 achieves benchmark-leading results on tasks like visual question answering and image captioning, with much less compute.
Its modular design allows rapid adaptation to new tasks and models, making it a practical choice for research and industry.
cards.caption: BLIP-2 sets new benchmarks while saving time and resources.

cards.title: Why BLIP-2 is a canonical paper
body: BLIP-2 showed that you can combine the best vision and language models without costly retraining, using a clever, lightweight bridge.
This approach has inspired a wave of research into modular, reusable AI systems that can adapt quickly to new challenges.

recap.label: Freeze
recap.note: Keep vision and language models unchanged.

recap.label: Bridge
recap.note: Add a query transformer to connect them.

recap.label: Train
recap.note: Teach the bridge using image-text pairs.

recap.label: Transfer
recap.note: Apply to new tasks with minimal extra effort.

cards.title: BLIP-2: Bootstrapping Language-Image Pre-training with Frozen Image Encoders and Large Language Models
body: Read the original paper for technical details and experimental results.
